common.terms-vc-id: Terms and Conditions Verifiable Credential ID
common.terms-c-subject: Terms and Conditions Credential subject ID
common.enable-encryption: Enable private key encryption through a compatible Webauthn security key
common.enable-signing: Enable signing with an eIDAS eID

tooltip.input-url: Public URL of a previously generated participant.json file. Must be HTTPS, publicly accessible and end exactly with '/participant.json'. Its content and structure will be validated.
tooltip.input-url-did: Public URL of the did.json identity file. Must be HTTPS, accessible and end exactly with '/did.json'. Its structure and required fields will be validated.
tooltip.legal-name: Full legal name of the organisation as registered. Must match exactly the commercial registry or legal certification. Max. 255 characters.
tooltip.legal-registration-number: Unique legal registration number for the selected type (vatID / taxID / EUID / EORI / leiCode). Will be validated against a trust registry. Format varies per type.
tooltip.headquarter-address: ISO 3166-2 code for the headquarters location. Format: [COUNTRY]-[REGION]. E.g. ES-28 (Madrid), DE-BE (Berlin), IT-RM (Rome). See wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-2
tooltip.legal-address: ISO 3166-2 code for the registered legal address. Format: [COUNTRY]-[REGION]. May differ from the headquarters if legally required. E.g. FR-75 (Île-de-France), NL-NH (Amsterdam).
tooltip.parent-organization: Optional. Name of the parent or holding organisation if your organisation belongs to a corporate group. Leave blank if this is a standalone entity.
tooltip.sub-organization: Optional. Name(s) of subsidiaries or dependent divisions. Separate multiple entries with commas.
tooltip.private-key: Private key in PEM format (PKCS8). WARNING — Security-critical: use the Generate button to create a new key pair, never share it publicly, and store it securely (HSM or Webauthn). Format: -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY----- ... -----END PRIVATE KEY-----
tooltip.doc-name: Descriptive and unique name for this identity document. Used in system records. Keep it short and meaningful, e.g. DID-2024-Main.
tooltip.issuer: Unique identifier of the credential issuer. Can be a DID (did:...) or an HTTPS URL. Must be verifiable and registered in the trust registry.
tooltip.vd-method: Cryptographic algorithm used to verify signatures. Common values: RS256 (RSA), ES256 (ECDSA P-256), EdDSA (Edwards-curve), PS256 (RSA-PSS). Must match the associated public key.
tooltip.vd-id: Globally unique identifier for this Verifiable Credential (URI). Must be unique, immutable and persistent. Typically a UUID (urn:uuid:...) or an HTTPS URL.
tooltip.vd-subject: Identifier of the entity the credential is issued to. Can be a DID or HTTPS URL. This field links the credential to the specific participant.
tooltip.terms-id: Unique ID of the Terms and Conditions Verifiable Credential. Same format as Verifiable Credential ID. References the VC containing the accepted terms.
tooltip.terms-subject: Identifier of the subject in the Terms and Conditions VC — who accepts the terms. Same format as Credential Subject ID.
tooltip.provided-by: Name or unique identifier of the service provider. Must correspond to a verified Gaia-X participant. Can be a DID or registered organisation name.
tooltip.policy: Persistent URL or URN referencing the service usage policy. Can be a PDF, a legal document or a URN. Must be publicly accessible.
tooltip.terms-url: Public, immutable HTTPS URL of the service Terms and Conditions. Must remain stable — any content change requires generating a new hash.
tooltip.terms-hash: SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the terms content. Prevents unauthorised modifications. Generate with: sha256sum file.txt or an online tool. Update whenever the terms change.
tooltip.format-type: Data format(s) returned by the service. Common formats: JSON, XML, CSV, RDF, Avro, Protocol-Buffers. Separate multiple formats with a comma.
tooltip.aggregation-of: If this service aggregates other services or offerings, list their IDs or references here. Separate multiple entries with a comma.
tooltip.depends-on: REQUIRED dependencies: services this offering needs to function (not optional). Critical for Gaia-X compliance. Separate multiple entries with a comma.
tooltip.request-type: Channel or method through which access to the service can be requested. Select the option your service supports.
tooltip.access-type: Type of service access: Digital = online/remote (APIs, web portals, etc.); Physical = on-site access at facilities.
tooltip.data-regime: Data protection legal framework: GDPR (EU), LGPD (Brazil), CCPA (USA), or another jurisdiction-specific framework.
tooltip.terms-checkbox: By checking this box you confirm you have read and understood the terms. You agree to: keep self-described data accurate, report any technical, legal or organisational changes, and comply with the Gaia-X Trust Framework. Inaccurate statements may result in credential revocation.

participant.terms-text:
The PARTICIPANT signing the Self-Description agrees as follows:
- to update its descriptions about any changes, be it technical, organizational, or legal - especially but not limited to contractual in regards to the indicated attributes present in the descriptions.

The keypair used to sign Verifiable Credentials will be revoked where Gaia-X Association becomes aware of any inaccurate statements in regards to the claims which result in a non-compliance with the Trust Framework and policy rules defined in the Policy Rules and Labelling Document (PRLD).
participant.terms-checkbox: I agree to the terms and conditions
participant.info-desc: Follow the steps to issue the Verifiable Credentials required to pass compliance. Once complete, you will get 3 Verifiable Credentials: Legal Participant, Legal Registration Number and Terms And Conditions.
participant.info-switch: I'll use my own DID solution
participant.has-file: Do you already have a participant file?
participant.has-file-did: Do you already have a DID file?
participant.activate-option: Activate this option if you want to import it directly via URL.

errors.error-lrn: An error occurred while validating the LRN
errors.error-get-json: An error occurred while retrieving the JSON file
errors.should-accept-terms: You must accept the terms and conditions
errors.invalid-headquarter-address: The headquarter address does not comply with the ISO 3166-2 format
errors.invalid-legal-address: The legal address does not comply with the ISO 3166-2 format
errors.invalid-private-key: The private key is not in a valid format
errors.accept-terms: You must accept the terms and conditions

step1.entry-empty: The input is not a non-empty string.
step1.invalid-path: The URL does not point to /participant.json.
step1.invalid-path-did: The URL does not point to /did.json.
step1.invalid-json: The content is not a valid JSON object.
